title: Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum
intro: Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum, also known as Purple Chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fever, as well as relieving headaches and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and fever-related conditions.
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chrysanthemum and is native to China. The classification of Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum is further divided into several classifications within the plant kingdom, including kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, and clade Eudicots. The plant is also classified within the order Asterales and family Asteraceae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum.
uses: Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum, also known as Purple-flowered Chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used to treat different ailments, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. It is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. The plant's extracts are used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum is used to balance the body's energy and promote overall well-being.

properties: Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum is traditionally used in Chinese medicine, and it's believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. It's also thought to aid in reducing fever and alleviate symptoms associated with the common cold. The plant's therapeutic properties are often attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum is used to treat conditions such as sore throats, coughs, and respiratory infections. constituents: Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum contains active constituents that include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds are thought to contribute to the plant's traditional uses in Chinese medicine. Additionally, Chrysanthemum purpureiflorum also contains phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the pharmacological properties of these constituents and their potential health benefits.
